questions.question: Show only action, user_id, log_id from the logs table.
questions.answer: SELECT action, user_id, log_id FROM logs;
questions.explanation: SELECT with specific columns returns only those columns.

questions.question: Write a CTE to filter products and then query it.
questions.answer: WITH filtered AS (SELECT * FROM products WHERE condition) SELECT * FROM filtered;
questions.explanation: WITH creates a temporary named result set.
questions.tip: CTEs make complex queries more readable and reusable.

questions.question: List all records from the order_items table.
questions.explanation: SELECT * returns every column and row from order_items.
questions.tip: Use specific columns instead of SELECT * in production.

questions.question: Count records in reviews grouped by comment.
questions.answer: SELECT comment, COUNT(*) FROM reviews GROUP BY comment;
questions.explanation: GROUP BY comment creates groups and COUNT counts each group.
questions.tip: All non-aggregated columns in SELECT must be in GROUP BY.

questions.question: Join the orders table with the payments table.
questions.answer: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN payments ON orders.id = payments.orders_id;
questions.explanation: INNER JOIN connects orders to payments on the foreign key.
questions.tip: Use table aliases to make queries more readable.
